settings.title: Ten Years in the Same Storefront
settings.excerpt: A decade ago we signed a lease on a shop most people thought was too small to last.
settings.body:
This summer marks ten years of Inkwell in the same little storefront on the corner.

We have tattooed grandparents and their grown grandkids, covered up regrets, and inked more than a few first time nerves into something someone is proud of. The walls hold a lot of stories now.

Ten years on one corner

People told us the shop was too small to last. It turned out small was the point. Staying in one place this long meant we got to watch the neighbourhood change around us and tattoo some of the same families twice over.

  • First tattoos that turned into full sleeves over the years.
  • Cover-ups that gave an old regret a second life.
  • Regulars who started as nervous walk-ins a decade ago.

To say thank you, we are running a charity flash weekend, with every booking going to the local crisis line that supports people in our community. It felt like the right way to mark ten years of being part of this neighbourhood.

How the weekend works

The flash sheets will be up the week before, and bookings open then. Every penny from the weekend goes straight to the crisis line, not a cut taken out for the shop.

Come for a piece, come to say hello, or come to see the photos we have pinned up from ten years of work. Whatever the reason, thank you for being the reason this corner is still here.
